factors.summary: The order backlog is growing, particularly within the Energy and Transportation segment, driven by increased external sales. However, there are expectations of lower order rates due to customers exercising capital discipline, despite strong quoting activity.

evidence.text: The order backlog increased primarily within the Energy and Transportation segment. Of the total backlog at March 31, 2024, approximately $6.0 billion was not expected to be filled in the following twelve months.
External Sales 5,487 $ 5,055 $ 432 9 %
While we continue to see a high level of quoting activity overall, we anticipate lower order rates as customers display capital discipline.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is strong and stable, with consistent positive operating cash flows and increased liquidity. The ME&T free cash flow is effectively utilized for financing activities, including debt repayments, dividends, and share repurchases.

evidence.text: ME&T free cash flow is a liquidity measure we use to determine the cash generated and available for financing activities including debt repayments, dividends and share repurchases.
Consolidated operating cash flow for the first three months of 2024 was $2.05 billion, up $479 million compared to the same period a year ago.
Enterprise operating cash flow was $2.1 billion in the first quarter of 2024.

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed with some areas showing growth and others remaining stable.

evidence.text: Sales increased across all applications except Industrial.

factors.summary: The company's debt leverage is stable, with minor fluctuations in specific debt components. While ME&T debt decreased slightly, Financial Products debt increased, indicating a balanced approach to maintaining financial stability.

evidence.text: Debt related to ME&T decreased $31 million in the first three months of 2024 while debt related to Financial Products increased $14 million.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are improving, driven by favorable price realization and cost management. Restructuring actions have contributed to margin improvements, with notable expansion in both gross and operating margins over the past year.

evidence.text: Restructuring income related to the divestiture of a non-US mining entity... We believe the non-GAAP measures will provide investors with useful perspective on underlying business results and trends.
segment profit as a percent of total sales was 19.5 percent in the first quarter of 2024, compared with 16.9 percent in the first quarter of 2023.
Operating profit margin was 22.3 percent for the first quarter of 2024, compared with 17.2 percent for the first quarter of 2023.

factors.summary: The company's volume trends show mixed performance across different segments, with growth in some areas and declines in others.

evidence.text: Unit volumes are growing, driven by higher sales volume and favorable price realization.
increase in sales was primarily due to higher sales volume of $231 million and favorable price realization of $202 million.
The decrease in sales volume was primarily driven by lower sales of equipment to end users; there was not a significant impact from changes in dealer inventories.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in Energy & Transportation, while other industries like Construction and Resource Industries are experiencing lower sales. This shift is reflected in an increase in segment profit as a percentage of total sales from 16.9% in 2023 to 19.5% in 2024.

evidence.text: segment profit as a percent of total sales was 19.5 percent in the first quarter of 2024, compared with 16.9 percent in the first quarter of 2023.

evidence.subsection: Highlights for the first quarter of 2024 include:

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is showing mixed signals. While the Energy & Transportation sector is experiencing a notable acceleration, with a 7% increase in total sales, other areas anticipate similar performance to the previous year, indicating a stable outlook.

evidence.text: Energy & Transportation’s total sales were $6.681 billion in the first quarter of 2024, an increase of $427 million, or 7 percent, compared with $6.254 billion in the first quarter of 2023.
For the full-year 2024, we continue to anticipate sales and revenues will be broadly similar to 2023.

factors.summary: The end markets show mixed momentum, with Energy & Transportation and Construction Industries growing, while Industrial and Resource Industries are experiencing slower growth.

evidence.text: Sales increased in large reciprocating engines, primarily data center applications. International locomotive deliveries were also higher.
In Construction Industries, we continue to expect North America to remain healthy in 2024...
In Energy & Transportation, we expect reciprocating engines and services for Oil & Gas to be about flat in 2024 after strong 2023 performance.
